_schema: One JSON object per line — the growth agent's structured engagement-experiment log (append-only). Fields: date (YYYY-MM-DD), rubric_lever (e.g. R1), hypothesis (one line), files (list of prompt files changed), commit (sha), predicted ({metric, dir:up|down}), baseline_note (measured baseline at ship time), status (running|measuring|promoted|reverted), verdict (null until settled). The agent skips any line containing _schema.
note: Seed line. Real experiments are appended below by the daily run.

hypothesis: Thumbnail hooks that stop echoing the title and instead open a curiosity gap (withhold the resolution, no title words) will raise CTR once impressions flow.
baseline_note: Render-and-judge: 9/12 golden-set hooks reused the title's distinctive words (ch2-code hook 'Um Servidor Para Tudo' a literal title slice; ch1-code '5 Lines Fix Slow RAG'). After change, title-echo ~1/12 with genuine gaps; both gated renders visible=True/no-fallback, verify_storyboard 38/38.
verdict: PROMOTED 2026-07-04 (SIGNAL-SCARCE: measured CTR still 0 on both channels — impressions not flowing, so no CTR cohort). Re-render-judged: today's baseline golden set shows 0/4 thumbnail hooks echo the title, all genuine curiosity gaps ('One tweak changes everything' vs title 'Fix Your Slow RAG'; 'Uma conexao que substitui mil' vs 'Um Servidor MCP Para Tudo'). Change holds; kept in code. Re-settle on measured CTR once impressions populate.

hypothesis: Portrait diagram beats waste ~55% of the 9:16 frame (landscape-ish viewBox width-fit to the frame); narrowing the viewBox so the stacked flow renders larger and fills more vertical space will improve mobile clarity and reduce mid-body dead-screen that drags retention.
baseline_note: Render-and-judge (measured retention is the confirmed weak spot: ch1 short avp 36.4%, ch2 21.4%, avd ~10s on ~35s shorts). Baseline ch1-code 4-node diagram filled ~51% frame width / ~38% height with large empty top. After (viewBox 1000->760, node 560->600): ch1-code 3-node diagram fills ~73% width and more vertical space, nothing clipped, both gated renders visible=True/no-fallback, verify_storyboard 40/40.
verdict: PROMOTED 2026-07-07 (SIGNAL-SCARCE: fix shipped ~12:00 UTC 07-04 and the mature cutoff is that same hour, so the mature post-fix retention cohort is ~empty — no SIGNAL-RICH verdict possible yet). Render-judged for the 3rd consecutive day: 07-07 baseline golden set shows portrait diagrams filling the frame (ch1-code 4-node stack ~62% width, ch1-concept 3-node large, nothing clipped). Change holds; kept in code. Re-check retention (avp of diagram-carrying videos) once the post-07-04 cohort matures with creation_config attribution.

hypothesis: Portrait cmp beats waste the 9:16 frame (two 42%-width side-by-side columns, small text, ~70% dead frame) and the absolutely-centered VS badge collides with card text; stacking the cards full-width with the badge in normal flow between them improves mobile clarity and cuts mid-body dead-frame that drags retention.
baseline_note: Render-and-judge (ch2 short avp 21.4%, ch1 35.7% — retention is the confirmed weak spot). Baseline ch2-concept/ch1-code cmp frames: narrow middle band, VS badge touching card text (ch2-concept). After: full-width stacked cards, badge in flow (collision structurally impossible), text ~2x larger, block fills ~35% of frame height; both gated renders visible=True/no-fallback; verify_storyboard 40/40.
verdict: PROMOTED 2026-07-08 (SIGNAL-SCARCE: every published video carrying creation_config is still <72h with 0 measured views, so no retention cohort). Render-judged on the 07-08 baseline: ch1-code b1_cmp and ch2-concept b5_cmp both full-width stacked cards, VS badge in flow between them, no text collision, large readable text. Change holds; kept in code. Re-check retention via creation_config beat-mix attribution once the cmp-carrying cohort matures.

hypothesis: Portrait diagram edge labels centered on the vertical connector get struck through by the line and can clip the node border above; placing them beside the line (start-anchored, gap-centered) with a paint-order stroke halo makes diagram beats legible on mobile and supports retention.
baseline_note: Render-and-judge: 07-06 baseline frames show the connector striking through 'context' (ch1-concept, label also clipping the Store box border) and 'protocolo' (ch2-code). After: ch1-concept 'store' label beside the line, centered in the gap, halo intact, nothing clipped; ch2-code diagram (unlabeled this render) unchanged-good. verify_storyboard 40/40; both gated renders visible=True, no fallback.
verdict: PROMOTED 2026-07-09 (SIGNAL-SCARCE: no elabel-carrying video has matured with creation_config attribution yet). Render-judged for the 2nd consecutive day: 07-09 baseline golden set shows edge labels ('20','5' on ch1-code Retrieve->Rerank->LLM) sitting cleanly beside connectors with a legible halo, no strikethrough. Change holds; kept in code. Re-check retention via creation_config beat-mix attribution once a diagram+elabel-carrying cohort matures.

hypothesis: Storyboards for videos ABOUT a tool/API/protocol/technique that include a concrete code/command beat (even when the narration never reads code aloud) carry more information per second and lift retention vs text-only beat mixes.
baseline_note: Render-and-judge: 07-07 baseline ch2-code (MCP explainer) had NO code and NO stat beat (hook,lst,term,cmp,diagram,stmt,quote,cta) — R2 scored 1, the only weak lever. After rule 2b: ch2-code mix hook,stat,term,diagram,code,cmp,quote,cta with a correct @mcp.tool() snippet and grounded 1x stat; ch1-concept control gained a sensible ctx-search/llm/db.save memory-loop snippet, no lever dropped. verify_storyboard 40/40, both gated renders visible=True no fallback.
verdict: PROMOTED 2026-07-10 (SIGNAL-SCARCE: no code-beat-carrying video mature with creation_config attribution yet). Render-judged on the 07-10 baseline, 3rd consecutive day: all 4 golden subjects carry a code beat (ch2-code MCP explainer: @mcp.tool() snippet + grounded stat; ch1-concept memory loop: save/recall/prompt snippet), varied mixes, R2 scored 2 everywhere. Rule 2b holds; kept in code. Re-settle on retention via creation_config beat-mix attribution once the code-carrying cohort matures.

hypothesis: Pinning idea-title and script language to the channel's render-profile voice stops wrong-language videos (3 EN videos shipped to the PT channel 07-07) and lifts ch2 retention/subs on affected topics vs those EN outliers.
baseline_note: Videos 495-497 (t10, ch2): English scripts narrated by pt-BR-AntonioNeural, published 07-07. Verified synchronously: generate_ideas(language='Brazilian Portuguese') returns PT titles; _generate_script(EN title, pt-BR voice) returns PT script; gated renders ch2-code (PT) / ch1-code (EN) clean, no lever dropped; verify_storyboard 40/40; live check: POST /api/topics/10/generate produced PT draft 543 through the restarted app.
verdict: PROMOTED 2026-07-11 (72h settle). Live-verified on the full post-ship cohort: all 21 ch2 videos published 07-08→07-11 have PT-BR titles (DB read-back this run), zero EN leaks; the one EN straggler (draft 541, generated pre-fix) was caught rejected and never rendered. Retention prediction (avg_view_pct up on affected topics) stays open — revisit via t10/t4 cohorts as they mature, but the defect the change fixed is gone in production.

hypothesis: Code beats whose body lines under a def/if/for/class lose their leading-space indentation (stripped by the shared _chars_clip helper) render syntactically-wrong Python on screen; preserving indentation via a dedicated code-line clip + an explicit LLM instruction fixes on-screen correctness and mobile clarity for code-carrying videos.
baseline_note: Render-and-judge: 2nd consecutive baseline (07-08, 07-09) showing ch2-code b_code frame with return api.get(q) flush-left under def buscar(q): — syntactically wrong Python on screen (R3 broken on this frame). Root cause: _chars_clip() unconditionally .strip()s every field including code.lines, destroying leading-space indentation even if the LLM emitted it; also added an explicit PRESERVE-indentation instruction to the code beat type doc. After: dedicated _code_line_clip() (rstrip-only) for code.lines — re-rendered ch2-code twice, return line now indented 2 spaces under def. verify_storyboard 40/40; gated render visible=True, no fallback; other levers (hook, cta) unchanged on re-check.
verdict: PROMOTED 2026-07-12 (72h settle, SIGNAL-SCARCE: no code-beat cohort matured with attribution yet). Render-judged 4th consecutive clean day: 07-12 baseline ch2-code b4_code shows `return db.run(sql)` indented 2 spaces under `def query(db, sql):` — syntactically correct Python on screen; nothing clipped on any read frame. Change holds; kept in code. Re-check retention via creation_config attribution once a code-carrying cohort matures.

hypothesis: Beat durations are emergent from LLM cue placement with no spacing guidance, producing sub-1.5s beats whose animations cannot complete (ch1-code diagram 1.02s with a half-faded node) and 8.7s drags; a cue-spacing PACING rule in the storyboard system prompt plus a deterministic ~2s tail floor in align_storyboard (pull the last two beats' starts earlier, stealing from a long predecessor) fixes frantic/draggy cadence and rescues the payoff+CTA from flashing by.
baseline_note: Render-and-judge: 07-10 baseline 3/4 subjects had a beat <=1.6s and/or >=8.5s (ch1-code: lst 8.7s then diagram 1.02s + cta 1.14s; ch1-concept: stmt 8.7s, quote 1.47s, cta 1.59s). Prompt rule alone was insufficient (2 renders: LLM kept anchoring quote+cta on the final words, 0.85s/0.97s); the tail floor closed it deterministically. After both: ch1-code diagram 2.1s (all 4 nodes render, vs ghosted LLM node at baseline), cta 2.22s, max drag 7.9s; ch1-concept quote 1.88s, cta 2.0s, mid-beats 2.0-5.7s. Unit-exercised align_storyboard on the exact failing timings (quote 0.854->1.88, cta 0.974->2.0, valid=True) + tiny-clip edge case stays pure word-sync. verify_storyboard 40/40; all gated renders visible=True, no fallback.
verdict: PROMOTED 2026-07-13 (72h settle, SIGNAL-SCARCE: R4 cohort not matured with attribution). Render-judged on the 07-13 baseline: zero beats under 1.64s across all 4 golden subjects (pre-fix: 0.85-1.14s flashes), every cta >=2.0s, tail floor holding (ch1-concept quote 1.88/cta 2.00 exactly at floor). Residual: the DRAG side persisted (ch2-code cmp 10.1s + lst 9.1s) — addressed same day by tightening the max cue gap 25->18 words (commit 1ba57be). Change holds; kept in code.

hypothesis: Reserving the day's first publish slot for a long-form (until one has published that quota day) gets ch1's buried approved longs out daily (FIFO never reached them behind 33 shorts) and lifts subscribers_gained — long-form drove 4 of the 5 sub-gaining videos ever.
baseline_note: ch1 published 5/5 shorts today with 3 approved longs waiting (directive-2 violation observed live). Verified synchronously pre-commit: _next_approved(ch1) flips FIFO short 545 → long 582 (has video_path); ch2 (long 532 already out today) unchanged. verify_publish 22/22, verify_storyboard 40/40; app restarted, dashboard 200. Watch: first ch1 publish ~00:00 UTC 07-12 should be video 582.
verdict: PROMOTED 2026-07-14 (72h settle). Mechanism live-verified on 3 consecutive days: ch1 published exactly 1 long + 4 shorts on 07-12 (long 582 first), 07-13 (583), 07-14 (584) — DB read-back this run; before the fix ch1 shipped 5/5 shorts with 3 approved longs buried. Predicted signal (subscribers_gained up) NOT yet visible: ch1 still 9 subs — longs 582/583 are <72h, cohort too thin; keep reading ch1 per-video subscribers_gained in coming runs. Residual: ch2 missed its long on 07-13 (5 shorts, 590-594) — watch whether the reserve logic skips ch2 when no long is approved by slot time.

hypothesis: A cta beat that is a FOLLOW ask (follow verb in the narration language) whose required sub-line names what the viewer gets NEXT from the channel (series part / tomorrow's topic / daily cadence) converts more viewers to subscribers than action CTAs or topic-echo sub-lines.
baseline_note: Render-and-judge: 07-12 baseline CTAs — ch1-code 'Try it → Add 5 lines today' (no follow verb), ch1-concept 'Follow → Build the memory layer' (action echo), ch2-code 'Segue → Um padrão pra tudo' / ch2-concept 'Segue → mais sobre RAG' (topic echoes); zero what-you-get-next rationale in 4/4 (R7=1, weakest lever; directive-4 priority, signal IS subscribers_gained). After: ch1-code 'Follow → Daily RAG fixes', ch2-code 'Segue → Como montar seu servidor MCP' (R7 1→2 both); hooks/code beats/closes intact, both gated renders visible=True no fallback, verify_storyboard 40/40.
verdict: PROMOTED 2026-07-15 (72h settle, SIGNAL-SCARCE: predicted signal IS subscribers_gained, but the post-07-12 published cohort with the new CTA is <72h/too thin to attribute — ch2's two sub-gaining videos 542/524 predate the change). Render-judged on the 07-15 baseline: 4/4 CTAs show a Follow/Segue verb + a concrete what-you-get-NEXT sub-line ('Daily RAG upgrades', 'Building agent memory next', 'Como montar seu MCP amanhã', 'Novo fix de RAG amanhã') — R7=2 holding. Change kept in code; re-check per-video subscribers_gained on the post-07-12 cohort as it matures.

hypothesis: The storyboard PACING rule's ~25-word max cue gap permits ~10s frozen cards (at ~2.5 w/s); tightening to ~18 words with an explicit 8s+=DRAG instruction (split the span with a stat/term/list) removes mid-body drags and lifts retention.
baseline_note: Render-and-judge: 07-13 baseline ch2-code held b3_cmp 10.12s + b6_lst 9.12s (~45% of a 42s video on two static cards), ch2-concept term 8.15s; R4 was the only lever scoring 1 (all others 2). After: ch2-code max mid-beat 6.74s, ch2-concept max 7.46s, no beat under 2.09s; frames clean (code indented, diagram elabel beside line, CTA follow+reason intact); verify_storyboard 40/40; both gated renders visible=True, no fallback. Caveat: after-render ch2-code hook ran 8.88s (long regenerated first sentence — hooks anchor to sentence 1; watch whether hook-length drag recurs).
verdict: PROMOTED 2026-07-16 (72h settle, SIGNAL-SCARCE) — WITH RESIDUAL. Kept: the tighter 18-word cue gap is directionally safe and the 07-14/07-15 renders were clean on ch2, but today's fresh baseline shows the prompt rule does NOT reliably hold: ch2-code regressed to a 10.44s cmp drag (pre-change baseline was 10.12s) and ch1 subjects kept sub-1.2s flashes the 93ec473 tail floor could not fix (bunched predecessors defeat it). R4 still scored 1 today. Conclusion: prompt-level pacing levers are exhausted; the flash half is now owned deterministically by the beat-floor backward relaxation shipped 2026-07-16. The drag half (a single long cue span the LLM will not split) stays open — revisit only with a content-aware mechanism, not another prompt rule.

hypothesis: Thumbnail hooks anchored in one concrete detail of the video's specific problem (while still withholding the resolution and avoiding title words) out-convert generic all-purpose bait phrases on CTR.
baseline_note: Render-and-judge: 07-14 baseline ch2-concept hook 'Ninguém te contou isso' was pure generic bait (fits any video; R8 the weakest priority-2 lever at 1, only other weak lever R4 priority 7 with a running experiment). After anchoring rule: ch2-concept 'Você cortou no lugar errado' (anchored to the chunking mistake, no title echo), ch1-code control 'Wrong answers killing your bot' (concrete symptom). R8 1->2, no lever dropped; verify_storyboard 40/40; both gated renders visible=True, no fallback. CTR still unmeasurable (impressions API) — settle SIGNAL-SCARCE via re-render-judge.
verdict: PROMOTED 2026-07-17 (72h settle, SIGNAL-SCARCE: CTR unmeasurable, render-and-judge is the evidence). 3rd consecutive judged day of anchored hooks: 07-17 baseline 4/4 anchored to the video's concrete problem with zero title echo — ch1-code 'Wrong answers were on top', ch1-concept 'Amnesia after every message', ch2-code 'Uma conexão substitui todas', ch2-concept 'Suas respostas vêm quebradas ao meio'. R8=2 holding since ship; no generic bait regression. Kept in code.

hypothesis: Publishing approved videos weight-first (Topic.weight DESC, then FIFO) gets strategy pivots to the audience while they are timely — concretely, ch1's operator-approved wedge cohort (t15/t5/t6) starts publishing 07-15 instead of ~07-23, producing measurable wedge conversion data before the 07-23 checkpoint and 08-01 pause decision.
baseline_note: ch1 wedge lead t15 (w3): 0 videos published since the 07-13 repositioning — 34 non-wedge shorts approved 07-08→07-10 sat ahead in strict FIFO. Verified synchronously pre-commit: _next_approved(ch1) flips 560 (t20 w1, approved 07-08) → 641 (t15 w3); ch2 → t4 (w3 winner). verify_publish 22/22, verify_storyboard 40/40, manager restarted (dashboard 200). Watch: ch1's 4 short slots on 07-16 should all be t15.
verdict: PROMOTED 2026-07-18 (72h settle). Mechanism live-verified via DB read-back: since 07-16 ch1 publishes weight-first — t15 Copilot wedge (w3) + t5 anchor (w2) lead every day (07-16: 4x t15 + 1 t5; 07-17: 2 t15 + 2 t5 + t6; 07-18: t5/t6), replacing the strict-FIFO backlog of 34 non-wedge shorts that buried the wedge cohort pre-fix; ch2 -> t4 (w3 winner) leads. Predicted signal (subscribers_gained up): ch1 moved 9->10 (first move off 9 in 11+ days — checkpoint directive-7 'ch1 finally moves past 9' met), but wedge-cohort attribution is still SIGNAL-SCARCE (post-07-16 published <72h). Kept in code.

hypothesis: A deterministic per-beat spacing floor in align_storyboard (2.0s tail, 1.8s mid; granted backward only while predecessors can fit their own floors) eliminates sub-1.7s beat flashes that prompt-level pacing rules (93ec473 tail floor, 1ba57be cue gap) could not reliably fix, improving R4 and retention.
baseline_note: Render-and-judge: 07-16 baseline R4=1, the only weak lever — ch1-concept tail floor FAILED (code 1.19s/quote 1.04s/cta 1.16s: four beats bunched in the last 3.4s defeat the two-beat floor via its predecessor guard), ch1-code term 1.09s flash, ch2-code code-beat 1.66s flash + 10.44s cmp drag. Unit-exercised the new floor on all three exact failing timing sets (quote 1.04->1.88, cta 1.16->2.0, zero beats under 1.68s, word-synced slack beats untouched, tiny-clip regression case byte-identical). Gated after-render ch1-concept: min beat 1.68s, cta 2.0s, no new drag, hook/CTA frames intact, visible=True no fallback, verify_storyboard 40/40. Manager restarted (dashboard 200). Residual: the DRAG side (single 10.4s cue span) is content-level and stays open.
verdict: promoted 2026-07-19 (SIGNAL-SCARCE, 72h+): floor effect confirmed holding in a fresh baseline render — ch1-code and ch2-concept both show min beat 1.68s, no sub-1.7s flashes, word-synced slack beats untouched. The KNOWN residual DRAG side (long uncapped hook hold: this run measured 8.33s ch1-code / 7.28s ch2-concept, ~27% of a short on one static card during the opening) remains open and is content/segmentation-level, not a deterministic-floor fix — logged as the next R4/R2 target.

hypothesis: The CTA's ask is R7's core signal (subscribers_gained), but the storyboard prompt rule for a follow-verb is violated ~half the time (07-18 baseline: ch1-code 'Try it -> Add 5 lines today', a pure action CTA with no follow ask; ch2-concept follow verb OK but topic-echo sub). Like the R4 pacing floor, making code deterministically force the cta text to the language's follow verb (LLM keeps authoring the sub) guarantees the follow ask on every render and lifts conversion.
baseline_note: Render-and-judge: 07-18 baseline R7 the weakest high-leverage lever (2/4 weak) — ch1-code 'Try it -> Add 5 lines today' (action CTA, NO follow ask, R7=1); ch2-concept 'Siga -> Conserte seu chunking' (follow verb OK but sub topic-echoes this video, R7=1); ch1-concept/ch2-code strong. After deterministic force (language via language_from_voice threaded into compose from worker + rubric_review): ch1-code CTA -> 'Follow -> Daily RAG fixes' (R7 1->2, follow ask guaranteed); ch2-code 'Siga -> Como montar seu MCP amanha' (PT verb correct, no English leak). Change touches only cta.text so no other lever affected; verify_storyboard 40/40; both gated renders visible=True, no fallback. Manager restarted, dashboard 200. RESIDUAL: the sub-line topic-echo (ch2-concept) is content-level and stays LLM-authored — revisit if it recurs. Predicted signal unmeasurable now (subscribers_gained attribution thin) — settle SIGNAL-SCARCE via re-render-judge unless a post-ship CTA cohort matures.
verdict: PROMOTED 2026-07-21 (72h+ settle, SIGNAL-SCARCE: subscribers_gained attribution still thin at 10/60 subs, no retention curve). Re-render-judged on the 07-21 baseline: 4/4 CTAs carry the deterministic follow verb + a concrete what-you-get-next sub-line -- ch1-code 'Follow -> Daily RAG fixes', ch1-concept 'Follow -> Building agent memory next', ch2-code 'Siga -> MCP na pratica amanha', ch2-concept 'Siga -> Novo fix de RAG amanha'. R7=2 holding since ship; no action-CTA or topic-echo regression. Kept in code.

hypothesis: The short-form opener is a long multi-clause wind-up (baseline: ch1-concept 22w first sentence holding the hook card 10.03s; ch2-code 30w) so the pain lands ~9-12s in, not <2s as R1 (PRIORITY 1) requires. Constraining the opening line to ~10 words, pain-first, no wind-up, lands the pain in the first 2s and shrinks the static hook-hold drag, lifting first-decile retention.
baseline_note: Render-and-judge: 07-20 baseline R1 the weakest high-leverage lever — ch1-concept opener 'You explain your whole project to your AI, close the tab, come back tomorrow, and it treats you like a total stranger' (22w, pain buried at end, hook held 10.03s DRAG + b1 1.68s flash); ch2-code 'Cada nova ferramenta que voce conecta na sua IA vira mais um codigo de integracao pra escrever, testar e manter...' (30w). After ~10-word cap: ch2-code first sentence 30w->8w 'Cansado de reescrever integracoes?' (hook 5.36s->4.44s); ch1-concept opener pain-first 'Your agent has amnesia' (hook 10.03s->6.08s, DRAG+flash both gone). R1 1->2 both, no other lever dropped (touches only sentence 1); both renders visible=True no fallback; verify_storyboard 40/40; manager restarted, dashboard 200. Predicted signal (avg_view_pct up on first decile) unmeasurable now (no retention-curve at low subs) -- settle SIGNAL-SCARCE via re-render-judge unless a post-ship cohort matures.
verdict: PROMOTED 2026-07-23 (72h+ settle, SIGNAL-SCARCE: no retention curve at 10/60 subs, avg_view_pct attribution on the post-07-20 cohort still too thin). Re-render-judged on both R1 subjects: ch1-concept opener 'Your AI agent has amnesia, and it's not a bug' (10w, pain in word 5; pre-change baseline was a 22w wind-up with the pain buried at the end) and ch2-code hook card 'Uma integracao nova toda vez' (PT, pain-first; pre-change 30w). Both renders visible=True, no fallback; R1=2 on both, and no other lever dropped — R7 CTAs still carry the deterministic follow verb + what-you-get-next sub ('Follow -> Agent memory build tomorrow', 'Siga -> MCP na pratica amanha'). The ~10-word pain-first opener cap holds 3 days post-ship; kept in code.

hypothesis: The visual CTA has carried a guaranteed follow verb since 70861ef, but the narration never asks. The 5 videos that ever gained subs all delivered deep specific value; an explicit spoken follow ask on the script's final line, welded to a named next-value in the channel language, converts exactly that moment and lifts subscribers_gained.
baseline_note: Render-and-judge: 07-24 baseline both languages close on the lesson line with NO spoken ask (ch1-code 'Don't retrieve more. Rerank better.'; ch2-concept 'Se o chunk nao faz sentido sozinho, sua RAG tambem nao vai fazer.'). First prompt draft produced a tease WITHOUT the ask ('Tomorrow I'll show you how to cache those reranker calls...') — directive tightened to require the explicit imperative + named next-value, both mandatory. After: 3/3 draft renders carry the ask, in-language, one line, natural — ch1-code 'Follow now, because tomorrow I'm showing you the chunking fix that makes reranking even sharper.'; ch2-concept 'Segue o canal, porque amanha eu mostro o tamanho ideal de chunk e o overlap certo.'; ch2-code 'Segue o canal, porque amanha eu mostro na pratica como subir seu primeiro servidor MCP do zero.' Visual cta sub now states the SAME promise the narrator speaks ('Tomorrow: the chunking fix' / 'Amanha: tamanho e overlap ideais'); storyboard rule 6 ending-plan re-anchored to the ask's first words (~10-16 from end; adversarial review caught the stale ~8-10 clause would have cued the card mid-ask). No other lever dropped: openers <=10w pain-first, min beat >=1.91s, cta holds 6.2-7.1s (<8s drag), visible=True no fallback x3. Suites 434 checks green. Manager restarted, /health ok 2/2, dashboard 200. RESIDUALS: cta sub silently clipped at 6 words if the LLM quotes instead of compresses; paragraph_number>=3 shorts can breach the 140w guard with the extra line (default n=2 lands ~100w). Attribution thin at current sub counts — settle SIGNAL-SCARCE via re-render-judge unless a post-ship cohort matures.
